figures.caption: Figure 3: Effects of hyperparamter of the 𝐾 value of Top—𝑘 retrieved items and 𝜆 value controled KL loss.

Figure 2: The overall framework of ReAd. (a) Online adaptation: The test user sequence is encoded by the encoder 𝑀𝜃 and used to retrieve the top-𝑘 most similar representations from the Collaborative Memory base. The retrieved representations are fused via the Retrieval Learning Module, then combined with the initial prediction 𝑝init to produce the final augmented prediction. (b) Offline retrieval learning: The retrieval representations are refined via a cross-attention layer, supervised by the recommendation loss Lrec and the alignment loss Lalign, with KL-divergence used to stabilize training. The red box indicates offline components, while the blue box corresponds to online inference.
Figure 5: The inference time considering batch inference and sample inference.

Figure 6: A case study on the MovieLens dataset showing retrieval results. Red dashed lines represent the test sequence and target item; blue dashed lines represent two retrieved sequences and their corresponding items.
Table 3: ReAd improvements on different backbones. All the results are significant with 𝑝-value < 0.01
